sound_type: fawn bleat -- thin high mew, contact call to mother. 500-1,500 Hz. Plaintive, wavering. The sound of vulnerability. E=3
ecosystem: Forest edge, meadow, suburban woodland, riparian corridor. Everywhere from deep forest to suburban gardens.
season: Fawns born May-June. Bleating strongest in first weeks of life. Year-round resident.

coordinates.description: Black-tailed Deer throughout western Washington. Forest edge, meadow, suburban. Ubiquitous.

taxonomy_note.description: FIRST CERVIDAE -- deer family enters. Marine mammal streak breaks at 15 (180-194). Columbian Black-tailed Deer, subspecies of Mule Deer. PNW's most common large mammal. Fawn bleat as infant contact call.
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Cervidae: Black-tailed Deer Fawn Bleat (195) -- FIRST.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Odocoileus from Greek 'odous' (tooth) + 'koilos' (hollow) -- hollow teeth. hemionus from Greek 'hemi' (half) + 'onos' (donkey) -- half-donkey (for mule-like ears). columbianus for the Columbia River region.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Odocoileus -- 2 species: White-tailed Deer and Mule Deer (including Black-tailed subspecies).
taxonomy_note.order_note: Artiodactyla. First artiodactyl in SPS. Even-toed ungulates.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Eighth mammal family in SPS: Phocidae, Otariidae, Mustelidae, Phocoenidae, Delphinidae, Balaenopteridae, Eschrichtiidae, Cervidae. First terrestrial-only mammal family.
taxonomy_note.class_note: Class Mammalia. Marine mammal streak breaks at 15 (180-194). FIRST CERVIDAE at 195.

song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: Fawn bleat: 500-1,500 Hz. Mother response grunt: 100-400 Hz.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Fawn bleat: 0.5-1.5 sec. Mother grunt: 0.2-0.5 sec.
song_metrics.note_structure: Single wavering bleat per call. Repeated at 3-10 second intervals.
song_metrics.note_types: Fawn bleat (contact call, plaintive mew), mother grunt (response, low reassurance).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Call-response: fawn bleats, mother approaches with low grunt. Simple two-voice dialogue.
song_metrics.timbre: Thin, high, wavering. Plaintive. Carries a few hundred meters in forest. Emotionally affecting.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=3. Quiet but audible. Infant mammal contact call. The sound of vulnerability and need.
song_metrics.tempo: Irregular. Bleats at 3-10 second intervals when seeking mother. Intensity increases with distress.
song_metrics.context: Forest edge, meadow. First weeks of life. Separation from mother. Hunger. Distress.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Minimal. Fawn bleats are structurally similar across Odocoileus populations.
song_metrics.key_property: FAWN BLEAT. FIRST CERVIDAE. Marine mammal streak breaks at 15. Thin high mew. 500-1,500 Hz. Vulnerability. E=3. v1.49.336.

pelage.overall: Fawn: reddish-brown with white spots (cryptic camouflage). Adult: grayish-brown in winter, reddish-brown in summer. White under tail (black on top -- the 'black tail').
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Fawn: white spots on reddish-brown (camouflage in dappled light). Adult: black-topped tail, medium size, large ears.
pelage.seasonal_variation: Summer: reddish-brown. Winter: grayish-brown, thicker coat. Fawn spots fade by 3-4 months.

physical_description.overall: Fawn: 3-4 kg at birth, 45-55 cm tall. Adult: 50-90 kg, 80-100 cm at shoulder. Medium deer. Black-topped tail. Large ears. Bucks with antlers (shed annually).
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Medium deer. Black-topped white tail. Large ears. Fawns spotted. Bucks with forked antlers.
physical_description.field_id: Medium-sized deer everywhere in western Washington. Reddish-brown (summer) or gray-brown (winter). Black-topped tail raised when alarmed. Fawns spotted, often found alone in vegetation (DO NOT DISTURB -- mother is nearby).

nodes.title: First Cervidae -- Deer Enter
nodes.description: Deer family enters. 50+ species worldwide. Black-tailed Deer is PNW representative. Marine mammal streak breaks at 15 (180-194).

nodes.title: Fawn Bleat -- Infant Contact Call
nodes.description: Thin high mew at 500-1,500 Hz. The sound of need. Calculated risk: attract mother vs. alert predators. 40-60% first-year mortality. E=3.

nodes.title: Ocean to Forest -- Habitat Transition
nodes.description: From 200-ton whales to 3-kg fawns. Deep ocean to forest edge. The engine returns to land. Scale collapse.

nodes.title: Marine Mammal Streak Ends -- 15
nodes.description: Degrees 180-194. 15 entries. 7 families. E=1 to E=7. Falls 2 short of Anatidae record (17). The marine mammal era is complete.

cross_references.relationship: INFANT PAIR! Seal pup whimper (E=3) and deer fawn bleat (E=3). Both infant contact calls. Both E=3. The energy of infant need.
SCALE COLLAPSE! Blue Whale 200 tons (E=5) to deer fawn 3 kg (E=3). Ocean to forest. The largest ever to a fawn.
FAWN-BUCK PAIR! Fawn bleat (E=3, need) and buck grunt (E=4, territory). Same species, degrees 195-196.
VULNERABLE AND SHORT-LIVED! Flop (6 years, frenzy victim) + fawn (40-60% mortality). Both vulnerable. Both brief.
